summary: Static QA audit of Stage 4 admin surface. Auth architecture is sound (three-layer defense, timing equalizer, server-only allowlist, PII redaction). Two P1 correctness bugs found in ToggleSwitch rollback announcement and a subtle timing-disparity risk. No P0 security holes. Admin pages missing skip-link; sitemap exclusion still a future TODO. Test infra still not scaffolded — catalog extended.

findings.issue: On server-action failure, the component rolls back `checked` to `prev` but unconditionally announces `srOff` via the aria-live region. If the user flipped OFF->ON and the action rejected, state correctly returns to OFF but the announcement for the previous ON state's rollback is 'Destaque desativado' — which is correct only by coincidence (because prev was OFF). If the user flipped ON->OFF and the action rejected, state returns to ON but the live region incorrectly announces 'Destaque desativado'. Screen reader users get wrong state.
findings.recommendation: Replace setLiveMessage(srOff) in the catch block with setLiveMessage(prev ? srOn : srOff) so the announcement matches the restored state.

findings.issue: Timing equalizer is a fixed 300ms sleep BEFORE redirect on non-allowlist, but the allowlisted path runs signInWithOtp which can take 200-900ms over WAN depending on Supabase region + SMTP queue. An attacker measuring wallclock over many samples can still side-channel allowlist membership (fixed 300ms vs variable 200-900ms produces distinguishable distributions). The handoff claims '±50ms tolerance' but real-world variance is much wider.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) fire-and-forget the signInWithOtp without awaiting (Supabase returns quickly; the email send is queued) so latency is bounded, or (b) use Promise.all([signInWithOtp, sleep(300)]) on the allowlisted path to enforce a minimum, and increase the non-allowlisted pad to at least 500-600ms to cover Supabase p95. Current implementation is better than nothing but is not 'indistinguishable'.

findings.issue: Admin pages (both /admin and /admin/login) do NOT render a skip-link. Public / has one, admin pages have a sticky h-16 AdminHeader and no way for keyboard users to bypass it. WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) is partially addressed by the <main> landmark but the .skip-link CSS class exists and isn't reused here.
findings.recommendation:
Add Pular para conteúdo above AdminHeader on both /admin/page.tsx and /admin/login/page.tsx, and set id='admin-main' on the
element.

findings.issue: rateMap is never pruned globally. underRateLimit() filters stale entries WITHIN an existing key's array, but keys whose entries all expire are never deleted from the Map. Over a long-lived Vercel lambda + abusive submission pattern, the Map grows unbounded with empty arrays. Also: the rate limit is keyed on normalized email only, so a single attacker can flood N distinct emails and never hit a single-email rate. Per-IP cap would complement this.
findings.recommendation: After filtering recent entries, if `recent.length === 0` delete the key. Optionally add a second Map keyed by `request.ip` with a lower ceiling (e.g. 20/15min). Noted in stage-4c open_questions as acceptable — keep as P2 until the allowlist grows.

findings.issue: POST /admin/logout has no CSRF protection. Next.js's built-in Server Action CSRF check does NOT apply to Route Handlers. A cross-origin form auto-submit could log the user out. Low impact (logout is not destructive to data) but violates defense in depth.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) convert logout to a Server Action invoked by the AdminHeader form instead of a Route Handler, or (b) verify request.headers.get('origin') / 'referer' matches request.nextUrl.origin before running signOut. (a) is cleaner.

findings.issue: Sort uses b.pushed_at.localeCompare(a.pushed_at) to achieve DESC. This works for ISO 8601 strings (which Supabase returns) but is fragile — if the DB ever returns a different format or null, localeCompare will either mis-sort or throw. The design spec says 'is_hidden ASC, pushed_at DESC', which page.tsx already fetches in that order — the resort is redundant against already-sorted data but needed because page.tsx orders by pushed_at only, not is_hidden.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add is_hidden to the page.tsx .order() chain and drop the client-side sort entirely, or (b) guard the comparator with `(b.pushed_at ?? '').localeCompare(a.pushed_at ?? '')`. Option (a) keeps a single source of truth.

findings.issue: On mobile (< md), the two toggle sections render the toggle AND a mono text label beside it ('destacar' / 'ocultar'). But the ToggleSwitch already renders a sr-only label (labelHidden=true). The visible mono label is not associated with the input (not a <label htmlFor>), so it's decorative — fine. But there's no visible label for the GitHub arrow anchor on mobile other than the aria-label — a visible 'github' caption would help mobile keyboard users.
findings.recommendation: Add a visible 'github' text on mobile beside the arrow to match the destacar/ocultar pattern. Minor UX consistency — not a WCAG failure.

findings.issue: submittedEmail comes from decodeEmail() in page.tsx which returns '' on corrupt base64. Page.tsx gates rendering on `sent && submittedEmail` (line 51), so a corrupt param falls through to the form. Good. BUT: an attacker crafting a link with `?sent=1&email=<malicious>` could pass an arbitrary decoded string (the regex/format is never re-validated here) and render it inside <strong> via React — React auto-escapes so XSS is blocked, but an attacker could produce a phishing-confirmation page at /admin/login?sent=1&email=<b64-of-attacker-crafted-string>. Harm is low (no auth action was taken) but the confirmation screen is convincing.
findings.recommendation: Re-validate the decoded email against EMAIL_REGEX in decodeEmail(); if it doesn't match, return '' so the form renders instead of confirmation. Also consider dropping the email echo entirely — the confirmation reads fine without it.

findings.issue: console.error for toggle failures dumps `error.message` directly. Supabase error messages sometimes include table/column names — fine for server logs but may leak schema detail to Vercel log aggregators.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for a single-admin app. If this ever becomes multi-tenant, wrap in a generic 'toggle_update_failed' log with redacted IDs.

findings.issue: Language fallback hardcodes 'TypeScript' when repo.language is null. Design spec didn't mandate a specific fallback; this implicitly assumes every repo is TS. A genuinely non-TS repo with a missing language column would mislabel.
findings.recommendation: Fall back to 'Unknown' or '—' instead of asserting TypeScript.

findings.issue: After success/error, phase auto-resets to idle after 3s. If the user tabs to the button at second 2.9, they see 'atualizado · 12 repos' and then the label changes under them. No aria-live suppression on this transition.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable — the aria-live region already announced the result. Consider pausing the reset timer when the button is focused.

happy_path_allowlisted_login_and_toggle: 1. User navigates to /admin. proxy.ts runs getUser() (no session). Redirects 307 to /admin/login with X-Robots-Tag: noindex.
2. /admin/login renders AdminHeader (no email) + MagicLinkForm. Metadata sets robots: noindex,nofollow.
3. User types 'mateushr23@gmail.com' and hits Enter. MagicLinkForm invokes requestMagicLink server action.
4. Action validates shape (passes), rate-limits (1/5 in window), isAllowlisted returns true.
5. headers().get('host')/get('x-forwarded-proto') build callback URL. supabase.auth.signInWithOtp sends email.
6. Action throws redirect('/admin/login?sent=1&email=<b64>'). MagicLinkForm form navigation lands on confirmation state.
7. MagicLinkConfirmation renders, FocusOnMount focuses 'enviar novo link' anchor. Screen reader announces 'enviar novo link, link'.
8. User opens email, clicks magic link. Browser hits /auth/callback?code=xyz.
9. Route exchanges code for session, isAllowlisted passes, redirects to /admin.
10. proxy.ts runs getUser() — now has user. No redirect. X-Robots-Tag set.
11. /admin page: createClient() reads session, isAllowlisted passes. createAdminClient reads all repos bypassing RLS.
12. AdminHeader renders with email in mono (truncated to 200px). RefreshButton + AdminRepoList render below.
13. User clicks featured toggle on row 3. ToggleSwitch handleChange flips local state OFF->ON immediately (optimistic), announces srLoading='Salvando…'.
14. startTransition fires toggleFeatured.bind(repo.id) — server action runs assertAdmin, updates row, revalidatePath('/admin'), revalidatePath('/').
15. Promise resolves, live region updates to 'Destaque ativado'. Thumb settles into the amber ON state. RSC re-renders with new row data.
16. User clicks refresh. RefreshButton onClick sets phase=pending, label becomes 'buscando no github…', ↻ glyph spins (unless reduced-motion).
17. refreshRepos runs assertAdmin then syncRepos() directly (no HTTP hop). Returns counters.
18. Phase=success, label 'atualizado · 15 repos' in sage success color. Live region says 'Atualizado: 15 repos.'. 3s later resets to idle.
denial_path_non_allowlisted_email: 1. Attacker navigates to /admin/login. Renders form. Types 'attacker@example.com', submits.
2. requestMagicLink: shape OK, rate-limit OK, isAllowlisted=false.
4. Confirmation page renders 'Se attacker@example.com estiver autorizado, o link foi enviado.'
5. Attacker never receives email. No distinguishing signal from the allowlisted path EXCEPT for the timing disparity flagged in P1 above (300ms fixed vs ~200-900ms for real signInWithOtp).
6. Alternate path — attacker somehow gets a magic link (e.g., typo, shared inbox). Clicks /auth/callback?code=xyz.
7. Route exchanges code successfully (Supabase creates user row). email = 'attacker@example.com'. isAllowlisted=false.

9. /admin/login?error=denied renders AuthDeniedBanner above MagicLinkForm. role=alert + FocusOnMount focuses banner. Message is generic ('Este e-mail não tem permissão de acesso.') — does NOT echo attempted email.
session_expired_back_to_admin: 1. User leaves /admin open for hours. Session cookie expires (Supabase default 1h access token, 1w refresh).
2. User returns, clicks a toggle. ToggleSwitch optimistically flips, announces 'Salvando…'.
3. Server action runs, getUser() fails silently (no user). assertAdmin throws new Error('not_authorized').
4. ToggleSwitch catch block: rolls back checked to prev, announces srOff. [P1 bug — should announce based on prev, not fixed srOff.]
5. User refreshes page. proxy.ts getUser() returns null. Redirect to /admin/login (fresh, no banner).
6. UX quality: no explicit 'session expired' message — user is silently bounced. Acceptable for a single-admin app but a toast 'sessão expirada, entre novamente' would be friendlier.

7. Edge case: if getUser() DOES return a user but the session email is no longer on the allowlist (admin removed themselves), /admin/page.tsx signs them out and redirects to /admin/login?error=denied. AuthDeniedBanner shows. User re-submits and lands back in the confirmation page forever. Effectively a lockout — intentional.

safeHref returns undefined for javascript:, data:, file:, vbscript:, chrome-extension:

safeHref returns undefined for malformed URLs that throw in new URL()
safeHref re-serializes (e.g. https://EXAMPLE.com/ normalizes host casing)
returns false for null, undefined, empty string
returns false when SUPABASE_ADMIN_ALLOWLIST env is unset
returns true for exact match (single entry)
returns true for case-insensitive match
returns true with surrounding whitespace on env entry
returns true for one of multiple comma-separated entries
redactEmail returns first char + ...@domain for valid email
redactEmail returns '<malformed>' for strings without @
redactEmail returns '<none>' for nullish
returns login_error_invalid for empty / non-email / >254 char
returns login_error_rate_limit after 5 submits in 15min window (same email)

rate-limit map prunes stale entries after 15min
redirects to /admin/login when ?code missing
redirects to /admin/login when exchangeCodeForSession fails
signs out + redirects to ?error=denied when email not allowlisted
redirects to /admin when email IS allowlisted

assertAdmin throws when session email not allowlisted

renders unchecked when defaultChecked=false
renders checked when defaultChecked=true

rollback on action rejection restores prev state (this test will catch the P1 above)

aria-live announces srLoading -> correct prev state on error (FAILS with current bug)

github-arrow anchor uses safeRepoHref (unsafe url hides arrow)

tabIndex=-1 and focused on mount
message does not include any passed email value
unauthenticated /admin -> redirect /admin/login with X-Robots-Tag
unauthenticated /admin/login -> passes through
authenticated /admin/login -> redirect /admin
authenticated /admin -> passes through with X-Robots-Tag
/auth/callback and /admin/logout bypass the gate
non-admin routes pass through without headers
visit /admin without session -> lands on /admin/login
submit non-allowlist email -> confirmation page (email echoed, form hidden)
mock magic-link callback for non-allowlist -> denied banner, email NOT echoed
mock magic-link callback for allowlist -> lands on /admin with AdminHeader + list
toggle featured on a row -> persists after reload
toggle hidden on a row -> row moves to end of list (sort invariant)

/admin/login passes axe wcag2aa
/admin/login?error=denied passes axe wcag2aa

different emails do NOT share the limit bucket (documents current behavior — also motivates IP-based limit)

coverage_note: No test infra exists yet. Stage 3 deferred Vitest + Playwright + axe-core scaffolding as a follow-up; that deferral carries through Stage 4. Coverage = 0% across the board. Creating the harness (vitest.config.ts, playwright.config.ts, src/test-setup.ts, src/mocks/handlers.ts, e2e/ directory, and the @axe-core/playwright dep) should happen in a dedicated 'test-infra' stage before or during Stage 5 review, then Stage 6 authors the tests from this catalog. 16 test files total; P1 priority (must-have for merge): 6 vitest + 2 e2e = 8 files.
